dsl_rule.translation_notes: Signal specifies 12-month lookback period. Mapped to sma_200 as the closest available long-term simple moving average (approximately 200 trading days ≈ 12 months). Entry conditions are directional based on price position relative to MA. Exit conditions trigger on MA crosses. Monthly rebalance frequency noted but implemented as 21-day max hold as proxy. CONTEXT_DEPENDENT direction preserved to allow both long and short positioning.

backtest_rule.reason: Signal specifies 12-month lookback period. Mapped to sma_200 as the closest available long-term simple moving average (approximately 200 trading days ≈ 12 months). Entry conditions are directional based on price position relative to MA. Exit conditions trigger on MA crosses. Monthly rebalance frequency noted but implemented as 21-day max hold as proxy. CONTEXT_DEPENDENT direction preserved to allow both long and short positioning.

source_rule_text: A trader should take long positions when price is above its moving average and short positions when price is below it, using a fixed lookback period to identify trend direction in futures markets.
